title: A Day in Sunset Harbour Miami
summary: Sunset Harbour Miami is unique. It is one of Miami Beach’s most popular, vibrant, and walkable neighborhoods. Located on the quieter bay side of South Beach, it trades the chaotic tourist energy of Ocean Drive for a chic, wellness-focused, local lifestyle. It packs a big…
whatHappening: Sunset Harbour is emerging as Miami Beach's premier alternative to the chaotic Ocean Drive scene. This bay-side neighborhood is attracting locals and visitors seeking a more refined, wellness-focused lifestyle in a walkable, vibrant setting.
whoInvolved: Miami Curated, a local lifestyle publication, is highlighting Sunset Harbour's appeal. The neighborhood itself comprises local businesses, wellness establishments, restaurants, and retailers that prioritize community over mass tourism.
whyMatters: As Miami Beach's tourism-saturated areas face overcrowding and quality-of-life concerns, Sunset Harbour's rise signals a shift in how travelers and residents prioritize experiences. This trend could reshape real estate values, business investment, and the broader identity of Miami Beach neighborhoods.
moreContext: South Beach has long been dominated by Ocean Drive's party atmosphere and tourist-centric commercialism. Sunset Harbour, positioned on the quieter bay side, offers a counterpoint by emphasizing local culture, wellness amenities, and walkability—reflecting broader travel trends toward authentic, health-conscious experiences. This neighborhood repositioning mirrors similar patterns in major tourist destinations worldwide, where secondary areas gain prominence as primary zones become oversaturated.

title: Taste the Gables is Back with 75 Restaurants

whoInvolved: The City of Coral Gables is organizing the event, partnering with a diverse array of restaurants ranging from Michelin-recognized fine dining establishments to casual neighborhood favorites representing varied cuisines.
whyMatters: The expanded event strengthens Coral Gables' position as a premier dining destination and drives foot traffic and revenue to local restaurants during a traditionally slower summer month. moreContext: Taste the Gables has established itself as an annual tradition similar to other major cities' restaurant weeks and months. The expansion to 75 restaurants demonstrates growing participation and confidence in the program's ability to attract customers. By clustering promotions during July, the city strategically counters typical summer tourism declines in South Florida.
